framework.name: Token of Authority Pattern for Agents
framework.description: Every autonomous agent action carries a cryptographic 'token of authority' — either user-invoked (immediate) or persistent delegation. Tokens are scoped (principle of least privilege), time-bound, and cryptographically verifiable. Enables audit trails and non-repudiation by binding each action to a human principal through digital signatures or token binding.
framework.raw_quote: At the heart of this framework is the idea that every autonomous agent action is a delegated action. The agent is either user-invoked ('schedule a meeting for me now') or operating under some persistent delegation ('manage my inbox every day'). In both cases, an agent should carry a token of authority — much like a power of attorney in the digital world.
